subject: Physics
title: The Zeroth Law
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Zeroth Law usable.

hook.text: The zeroth law of thermodynamics was so fundamental it had to be inserted before the first law.

buildup.text: It states: if object A is in thermal equilibrium with object C, and B is too, then A and B are in equilibrium.

discovery.text: This sounds obvious but it's the reason thermometers work. The thermometer reaches equilibrium with your body.

twist.text: The law was formulated in the 1930s—decades after the first and second laws were already named.

climax.text: Without the zeroth law, temperature itself wouldn't be a well-defined, measurable quantity.

punchline.text: So basic it needed the number zero. The law that makes thermometers possible.

quiz.question: What does the zeroth law of thermodynamics establish?
options: That thermal equilibrium is transitive, making temperature measurable
That energy cannot be created or destroyed
